label: Heavy going
summary:
A punishing day on multiple fronts. At least 21 people were killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon — including two Syrian children and a paramedic — while in Gaza a Palestinian family of five was burned alive when an Israeli airstrike hit their home overnight. Hezbollah formally rejected the ceasefire, and Iran's IRGC warned there will be no peace until Israel withdraws from Lebanon. The WFP warned the Strait of Hormuz closure has pushed an additional 6 million people into acute hunger across Somalia, Sri Lanka, and Afghanistan.

Markets cratered: the Nasdaq plunged 4.18% — its worst day since April 2025 — as a hot jobs report (172K vs 88K expected) sent the 10-year yield above 4.5% and the 30-year past 5%, sparking a $1 trillion semiconductor selloff. Oil fell 3% on weakening Chinese demand. World Environment Day was marked by sobering warnings: the UN said there is an 86% chance at least one year before 2030 will surpass 2024 as the hottest ever. The Trump administration scrapped ocean sensors and announced $700M for coal. The only bright spots: an AI-designed universal coronavirus vaccine passed its first human safety trial, and octopuses were found to use mirrors — a cognitive feat previously seen only in vertebrates.

drivers.headline: At least 21 killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon; family of five burned alive in Gaza airstrike
Hezbollah formally rejects ceasefire; Iran's IRGC warns no peace until Israel withdraws from Lebanon
WFP warns Strait of Hormuz closure has pushed 6 million more people into acute hunger across Somalia, Sri Lanka, and Afghanistan
Nasdaq plunges 4.18% — worst day since April 2025 — as hot jobs report sends yields soaring; $1T wiped from chip stocks
US employers added 172K jobs in May vs 88K expected; 10-year yield jumps above 4.5%, reviving Fed rate hike fears
World Environment Day: UN warns 86% chance a year before 2030 will be hottest ever; El Nino forming
Trump administration scraps ocean sensors and announces $700M in federal funding for coal industry
Senate approves $70B for ICE and Border Patrol; Chinese crude imports fall to 10-year low signalling demand weakness
AI-designed universal coronavirus vaccine passes first human safety trial — found safe and well tolerated
Octopuses found to use mirrors to locate hidden food — cognitive feat previously seen only in vertebrates
Record 603 dams removed across Europe in 2025, reconnecting 2,300 miles of waterways for fish migration
